metadata.notes: Live re-recorded 2026-07-15: /rewind forks a new Kiro session. The original JSONL remains intact; the child sidecar has session_created_reason=rewind and top-level parent_session_id. The daemon observes a new ready session, removes the original row, then tracks the post-rewind turn on the child.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: partial
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

Kiro CLI 2.6.0 implements /rewind as a conversation fork, not an in-place truncation. The picker creates a second ~/.kiro/sessions/cli/<uuid>.jsonl whose sidecar has session_created_reason=rewind and parent_session_id=<original uuid>. The original JSONL keeps both pre-rewind turns; the fork contains the selected checkpoint and the subsequent turn.

## Observable lifecycle

The live recording shows two normal turns on the original session, then transcript_new and ready for the rewind-born session. The watcher emits transcript_removed for the original while its file is still present on disk, so the fixture records that removal rather than treating it as file deletion. The next prompt runs ready → working → ready on the new session. This fulfills the scenario's linked-new-session arm; the current adapter does not project the sidecar parent link as ParentSessionID, so the lifecycle assertion is session birth and post-fork behavior rather than parent_linked.

The capability is partial because /rewind restores conversation context only; it does not demonstrate restoration of prior filesystem edits. Kiro's JSONL still has no token or cost totals for the adapter to read.
caveats: The original transcript file remains intact even though the daemon emits transcript_removed for its row at the fork. The removal is a watcher/PID lifecycle observation, not disk deletion.
parent_session_id is proven in the rewind sidecar. It is not currently emitted by the adapter as ParentSessionID, so no parent_linked event is expected.
V3 storage is not assessed here: this machine has Kiro CLI 2.6.0, whose supported engine flags are v1, v2, and kas rather than the later --v3 surface.

sources.note: Live 2.6.0 lifecycle: original session, rewind-born session, original transcript_removed, then child ready→working→ready.
rewind_fork waits for the sidecar relationship and includes both transcripts in the curated fixture.

preconditions: kiro-cli 2.6.0 or later is installed, authenticated, and has trust-all-tools consent.
The recording daemon runs with all permissions granted.
setup: The interactive driver launches one Kiro TUI in an isolated cwd and records the original transcript.
After picker confirmation, rewind_fork discovers the child sidecar by parent_session_id and retains both source transcripts.

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: one
Reply with exactly the word: two
Reply with exactly the word: three

verify: The original session reaches ready after one and two.
The fork creates a distinct session with a parent_session_id sidecar and the daemon records its ready birth.
The original session is removed from the daemon lifecycle without its JSONL disappearing from disk.
Three runs ready→working→ready on the forked session.
